strategy: Attack with Life. Dewey takes +30% Life and resists Death 62% and everything else 32%.
Every time you damage him he shields himself in that school, so chipping gets walled. Funnel blades to one hitter and land a single overwhelming Life hit instead of many small ones.
Do not cast damage-over-time: any DoT triggers Mass Triage, healing his side and cleansing it.
Keep single blades to two. A third individual blade drops Bad Juju on the last one. Use Bladestorm or Aegis to stack safely.

Never let him sit on four or more shields at end of round, when he casts Orthrus on the top-threat wizard and converts shields into two deferred DoTs. Use Pierce and quick kills so shields never pile up.
Join on round 1 to dodge the late Mana Burn, then burst him before his minion so the fight stays simple.
cheatNotes: Join on round 1; arriving late costs you 2 pips to a Mana Burn.
Every damage spell makes him drop a matching Shield on himself, blunting your next same-school hit. Hit once, big, rather than chipping.
Multi-hit spells give him a shield per hit, stacking his defense fast.
Balance attacks give him a Tower Shield instead, so off-school hitting does not dodge the shield.
Any DoT you cast triggers Mass Triage, healing his side and stripping the DoT. Leave DoTs out of your deck.
Casting three separate single blades makes him Bad Juju the third recipient. Keep to two singles or use Bladestorm and Aegis.
If Dewey dies before his minion, the Bad Juju cheat stops firing.
Four or more shields on him at round's end triggers Orthrus on your top-threat wizard plus two deferred DoTs. Use Pierce and quick kills so shields never pile up.
